name: Oasis

earnings.assumptions: Estimate assumes outsized writer economics around Noel Gallagher-led composition plus continued streaming, live-culture nostalgia, and sync demand.

ownership.master_owner: Masters appear substantially label-controlled on the core catalog
ownership.publishing_owner: Publishing value is concentrated around songwriter-controlled interests
ownership.catalog_sale_status: No confirmed full catalog sale assumed in this estimate
ownership.notes: Oasis likely earns a meaningful songwriter-side cut even where label participation remains strong on the master side.

bio: Oasis became one of the defining British bands of the 1990s, turning singalong rock songs into a globally durable catalog.
notable_for: Anthemic choruses, Britpop-era dominance, and long-tail catalog demand.
career_highlight: Their 1990s catalog still drives global streaming, nostalgia demand, and licensing value.
revenue_drivers: Catalog streaming continues to monetize Oasis long after the original release cycle.
Nostalgia playlists keep their biggest songs in constant rotation.
Licensing and sports/event usage reinforce the band's anthem-like tracks.
